desc: Rome layers imperial ruins, Renaissance art, and everyday neighborhood life across a city that still feels monumental and lived-in at the same time. Beyond the Colosseum and Vatican, the appeal is wandering piazzas, trattoria districts like Trastevere and Testaccio, and a constant mix of archaeology and contemporary city energy.
hls: World-class ancient sites: Colosseum, Roman Forum, Pantheon
Vatican Museums and St. Peter's Basilica masterpieces
Walkable neighborhoods with distinct character (Trastevere, Monti, Testaccio)
Iconic piazzas and fountains from Piazza Navona to Trevi
Outstanding museum depth from Capitoline to Borghese
Frequent high-speed rail links for day trips to Florence or Naples

todo.name: Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill
todo.desc: Book timed entry to Rome's core ancient complex and allow at least half a day.

todo.name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
todo.desc: Reserve ahead for one of Europe's top collections and Michelangelo's frescoes.

todo.name: St. Peter's Basilica dome climb
todo.desc: Climb for panoramic views and tour the basilica's monumental interiors.

todo.name: Pantheon and Piazza Navona walk
todo.desc: Link two signature landmarks with cafés and Baroque streets in between.

todo.name: Borghese Gallery
todo.desc: See Bernini sculptures and Caravaggio works with mandatory timed tickets.

todo.name: Trastevere evening food crawl
todo.desc: Sample Roman classics such as cacio e pepe, supplì, and artichokes.

todo.name: Appian Way and Catacombs
todo.desc: Rent a bike or take a guided route along ancient paving and early Christian sites.

todo.name: Baths of Caracalla or Capitoline Museums
todo.desc: Choose between vast Roman bath ruins or curated sculpture collections near the Forum.

wifi.note: Generally solid in hotels and apartments; coworking options are extensive in central districts.

prac.fltNote: London Gatwick has frequent nonstop service to Rome year-round, typically to Fiumicino with additional options depending on season.
prac.visa: Italy is in the Schengen Area; many non-EU visitors (e.g., US/UK) can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free.

sweet: April to June and late September to October are ideal for ruins and piazzas outside peak summer heat.
